findings.title: Day view header lies about contents — it's a list, not a swim-lane grid
findings.detail: The codebase markets DAY as 'per-crew swim lanes on a 20-min grid' (Scheduler.jsx:5, 519) and SchedulerPreview.jsx renders exactly that, but the live DayView (Scheduler.jsx:744-946) renders a single flat run-sheet list of SortableJobCards (line 911-922). HourRuler, CrewLane and JobBlock components (lines 619-663) are defined and unused. A user who watches a demo of the gorgeous swim-lane preview and then signs up will get a different product — and a multi-crew operator (Britannia's 1500 staff scenario) has no way to see crews side-by-side. The crew filter pills above (line 2392) suggest crews exist as a dimension, but selecting one only filters the same list rather than showing lanes.
findings.fix: Either (a) restore the multi-lane DayView from SchedulerPreview when >1 crew is active and fall back to the run-sheet for single-cleaner accounts, or (b) delete the dead lane components and re-message the marketing copy. The cleaner-friendly answer: per-crew lanes on desktop, swipeable per-crew run-sheet stack on mobile.

findings.title: Week view silently drops every job — `j.day` is never set on real data
findings.detail: WeekView reads `j.day` to bucket jobs (Scheduler.jsx:964-971): `if (j.day >= 0 && j.day < 7) map[j.day].push(j)`. Real jobs from Supabase have a `date` string, no `day` integer. The root component does compute it at line 2280-2285 (`weekJobsWithDay`) and passes it in, so it works — but it ONLY works because of that one mapping. The instant anyone refactors WeekView to receive `weekJobs` directly (which the type filter logic and demo data both assume), the entire grid silently shows empty. Worse, when day = 0 (Sunday legacy data) and `dayOfWeek === 0` it gets remapped to index 6 — meaning historical CleanerPlanner-imported Sunday jobs may quietly land on the wrong column.
findings.fix: Compute day-of-week from `date` inside WeekView so it's self-contained, and add a dev-only assertion when `j.day` is undefined. Use a stable date-keyed Map rather than a 7-slot array so DST/locale don't bite.

findings.title: Drag-to-reorder destroys real start times
findings.detail: On drag end (Scheduler.jsx:817-842) every incomplete job's start_hour is rewritten to `baseHour + i * 0.01`, i.e. 36-second gaps. The Day drawer then displays `Time 08:00 — 08:00` (line 1472) because durations are 30+min but starts are 36s apart. The swim-lane DayView (when it's used, line 552-603) positions jobs by `(startHour - DAY_START) * PX_PER_HR`, so after one drag every job collapses into a 1px stack. Week view (`layoutDayJobs`, line 263) uses the same data and will mis-overlap. A cleaner who 'just wanted to swap job 3 and 4' will look at their week and see chaos.
findings.fix: Persist sort order in a separate `display_order` column or as `sort_index` JSON on the job — don't fight start_hour. If the goal is to keep times meaningful, snap to the original time slots and only swap the times between the two reordered jobs.

findings.title: 'Route' button opens Google Maps with NO origin — drives cleaners to first customer instead of from home
findings.detail: `handleRoute` (Scheduler.jsx:797-801) builds `https://www.google.com/maps/dir/{pc1}/{pc2}/...` — no starting point. Google Maps interprets the first stop as the origin. A window cleaner with a profile.home_postcode set will still get a route that 'starts' at their first customer, hiding the morning drive-to-first-job (often 20+ min). Auth already exposes `home_postcode` per the memory file. Also: postcodes go in raw — if any contain a space (W8 4AA), the URL works but if any are missing/empty, the URL silently breaks with `//`.
findings.fix: Prepend `profile.home_postcode` (fallback: first job postcode) as the explicit origin. Add an end-of-day return-to-base toggle. Drop trailing `/` segments. Bonus wow: show the optimised order with estimated drive time and a 'Save 14 minutes — reorder?' banner above the run-sheet (this is the uniquely cleaner-industry insight competitors don't show).

findings.title: Empty state is the loudest missed wow-moment in the product
findings.detail: DayView empty state (Scheduler.jsx:846-855) is one sad line: 'No jobs scheduled for this day. Use the "+ New job" button to add one.' A first-time user lands on Day view (the hero) and sees this on day 1. Compare to Cleaner Planner / Squeegee — they show a 'how rounds work' explainer; Jobber shows a sample job. Cadi has demo data baked in (buildDemoJobs, line 83) and a Rounds importer, but doesn't surface either from the empty Day. The Rounds tab has a much better empty pattern (line 2094-2101) — that energy belongs on Day.
findings.fix: Make empty Day a celebration moment: '🌤️ Nothing booked for Thursday — fancy a day off, or shall we...' with three big chips: 'See demo day (the Kensington round)', 'Import my customers from CleanerPlanner', 'Add a one-off'. Same treatment for empty Week. This is also where AskCadi could proactively offer 'Want me to schedule your fortnightly round for this week?'

findings.title: Rounds 'Schedule' button is a dead end — uses round name as customer name
findings.detail: Per the mapper and confirmed at Scheduler.jsx:2245-2249, clicking 'Schedule' on a Friday — SW19 Exterior round opens NewJobModal with preCustomer = 'Friday — SW19 Exterior'. A user expects 'schedule this round = create all 3 customer jobs sequentially for next due date'. Instead they get a blank form with a weird customer name typed in. For an exterior window cleaner — Cadi's stated wow audience (memory says exterior round of 25 customers in 20-min slots) — this is THE feature. Right now it's a brochure-only promise.
findings.fix: Replace the modal with a 'Schedule round' preview sheet: shows all active customers as draggable cards in suggested order (postcode-clustered, longest leg first), pre-fills date/duration/price from round metadata, lets user pick crew + start time once, then writes N jobs in one Supabase batch. End-of-flow animation: cards fly into the Day timeline one-by-one. This is the moment that beats Squeegee.

findings.title: Generic time-grid where Cadi could own a unique 'round timeline'
findings.detail: Day, Week, Month, Quarter are all standard SaaS calendar views — every competitor has them. The 20-min slot system, route-aware ordering, and per-customer due dates from the Rounds data model are unique to Cadi but invisible on the timeline. JobBlock (line 552-603) uses pastel by type, but there's no visual cue for: 'this customer's last visit was 9 weeks ago — they're overdue', 'driving 12 min from previous job', 'this is an Airbnb turnover with a 11am hard deadline'.
findings.fix: Inject three uniquely-cleaner overlays on the Day timeline: (1) thin grey 'drive time' gap blocks between jobs computed from postcode haversine, (2) a red left-edge pulse on overdue rounds customers, (3) a clock-icon badge on Airbnb/end-of-tenancy jobs with hard deadlines. None of Jobber/ServiceM8/Squeegee do this combined. This is the screenshot that goes on the marketing site.

findings.title: Day view celebration is muted — 100% complete just shows '🎉 All done!'
findings.detail: Line 866: `{done === total && total > 0 ? '🎉 All done!' : ...}`. That's the entire end-of-day moment. A van-based exterior cleaner who's just finished 25 houses in the rain wants a hero moment: today's takings counted up (the count-up animation IS already there at line 790 — but only for partial), a 'best day this week' badge, a 'send all 25 invoices' one-tap CTA, a share-to-WhatsApp 'just finished my round' card.
findings.fix: On done===total swap the progress strip for a full-bleed celebration card: animated £ total, jobs completed, miles driven, a confetti burst on mobile (one-time per day, gated by localStorage to avoid annoyance), and a 'Invoice all £X' CTA wired to the Stripe billing or the Britannia invoice workflow.

findings.title: NewJobModal recurrence balloons up to 12 future jobs with no opt-out
findings.detail: Line 1776-1788: when recurrence != 'none', the modal silently creates 11 additional jobs (`for i < 11`). The 'next occurrence' hint (line 1986) only shows the FIRST one. A first-time user picks 'Fortnightly' assuming Cadi will roll one forward each time it's completed — instead they get 12 jobs littering their Month view, all editable but none labelled as part of a series. There's no 'edit series' affordance later, and no series_id stored on the job (line 1762-1772) so changing one customer's price later won't propagate.
findings.fix: Either (a) show a clear 'Schedule 12 occurrences' preview chip with a number-picker, OR (b) switch to a recurrence rule on the customer (the recommended pattern — Rounds already implies this) and generate jobs lazily 4 weeks ahead. Add a `series_id` so the drawer can offer 'Edit this · Edit series'.

findings.title: Mobile search is missing — and mobile IS the cleaner's primary device
findings.detail: Search input is `hidden md:block` (Scheduler.jsx:2350). A cleaner on a van iPhone trying to find 'Mrs Davies' from a missed call has no search at all. There's a MobileDayStrip (line 476-517) but no mobile search affordance. Filter pills are also wrap-heavy on small screens with no scroll containment.
findings.fix: Add an icon-only Search button next to the New Job button on mobile that pops a full-width sheet with recent customers + voice input. Make the filter row horizontally scrollable rather than wrapping.

findings.title: Status updater asks user to think in jargon, not verbs
findings.detail: JobDrawer (line 1487-1502) shows four equally-weighted square buttons: Scheduled, In progress, Complete, Unassigned. For an in-van cleaner the question is always 'have I started?' or 'am I done?'. Forcing a four-way state picker on every job is a tap tax.
findings.fix: Make the drawer header a single big context-aware button: when scheduled → 'Start job ▶', when in-progress → 'Mark complete ✓', with a small overflow menu for the rarer states. Add swipe-right-to-complete on the run-sheet cards (the SortableJobCard at line 666-742 already has a Done button column — just promote it to a swipe gesture). Combined with the count-up £ animation this becomes Cadi's signature one-handed-in-the-van flow.

findings.title: Accessibility: drag handles unreachable by keyboard, status pills colour-only
findings.detail: (a) SortableJobCard (line 666-742) uses dnd-kit but only attaches PointerSensor + TouchSensor (line 810-813). KeyboardSensor isn't installed — keyboard users cannot reorder jobs at all. (b) JobBlock 'isComplete ? opacity 0.55 + line-through' (line 572) is fine, but the unassigned indicator is a 6px red dot (line 597-599) with no text alt, no aria-label, and red-on-pastel-orange/green fails WCAG. (c) StatusChip 'Now' (line 608) uses colour as the only differentiator from 'Booked'. (d) The whole grid background is glassmorphism with backdrop-blur and white-on-white shadows — text contrast on the 0.55-opacity completed jobs is roughly 3:1 against the gradient. (e) Modal close button (line 1823) is a 18px X with no aria-label; same for drawer (line 1438).
findings.fix: Add KeyboardSensor + visible focus rings on cards (currently none). Add aria-labels to all icon-only buttons (X, Trash2, Pencil, GripVertical). Pair every colour-coded status with a glyph (✓ for complete, ▶ for in-progress, ! for unassigned). Up opacity floor to 0.7 and darken ink for complete state. Test the day-strip with VoiceOver — `aria-current=date` is missing.

findings.title: MonthView dot row collapses 25-job exterior days into 6 indistinguishable dots
findings.detail: Line 1204-1208: `cell.jobs?.slice(0,6).map(...)` shows max 6 dots then '+19'. On the hero exterior round day this hides the at-a-glance density that's the whole point of a month view. Also all dots are same size regardless of revenue — a £25 windows job looks like a £280 end-of-tenancy.
findings.fix: Replace dots with a single mini sparkline-style stacked bar per cell (type slices sized by count + saturation by revenue). Or borrow GitHub's contribution-graph idea: cell background tint by total £, with type indicator strip top-right. Adds genuine information density.

findings.title: Filter state survives view-switch and silently hides jobs the user can't see why
findings.detail: `typeFilter` / `crewFilter` (line 2220-2221) are global to the tab. A user filters to 'Jamie' in Day view, switches to Month, and now their month silently excludes everyone — but Month/Quarter (line 1143-1382) don't render the filter bar (only Day does, line 2374-2401). The stat strip at the top still respects the filter (line 2370) so revenue figures lie. No 'X jobs hidden' banner.
findings.fix: Either reset filters on view change, or persist them visibly with a small 'Filters: Jamie · Exterior — clear' pill above the view content regardless of view.

findings.title: First-five seconds: no progressive disclosure, no 'what is this'
findings.detail: On first load with demo data, a new user sees: gradient orbs, glass cards, 13 stat numbers, 5 view tabs, 5 filter pills, 5+ crew pills, 20 job cards, AskCadi at the bottom. There's no welcome state, no spotlight, no 'Tap a job to see details'. Onboarding (per memory) covers chat onboarding but the Schedule tab itself has no first-run coach-marks.
findings.fix: Add a one-time tour scoped to the Schedule tab: highlight 'tap a job → details', 'drag to reorder your run', 'tap Route for sat-nav'. Trigger from the Ready banner on Dashboard mentioned in onboarding memory. Three steps max.

findings.title: Weather + traffic = the killer wow Cadi could ship in a week
findings.detail: Exterior cleaners (Tom's softwash + Jamie's window round in the demo data) live and die by weather. Currently nothing in DayView, WeekView or the New Job modal references weather. A single Met Office API call keyed off home_postcode + date could add a tiny ☔️ icon to any exterior job at >60% precipitation forecast, with a 'Suggest reschedule' chip. None of Jobber / ServiceM8 / Squeegee / Cleaner Planner do this. This is exactly the kind of uniquely-cleaner moment the brief asks for.
findings.fix: Met Office DataHub (free tier) → cache 7-day forecast per postcode → render a weather strip across the Week view header (one icon per day) + a per-job warning badge for exterior jobs. The 'reschedule rain day' confirmation that drags the whole day forward to next dry day = the screenshot moment.

findings.title: No 'what changed' feedback after save — silent success teaches mistrust
findings.detail: Drawer save (line 1394-1405) closes the panel on success with no toast. New job modal (line 1730-1791) same — modal closes, you blink and a new card appears (or doesn't, if the Week is wrong). Delete (line 1407-1415) is irreversible with no undo. For a cleaner adding 12 fortnightly jobs at once this is terrifying.
findings.fix: Bottom-of-screen toast 'Job saved · 12 occurrences scheduled — Undo' with a 6-second timer. Same pattern for delete. Wire to a simple `pendingDelete` queue so 'undo' actually un-deletes within the window.

findings.title: Scheduler.jsx is a 2449-line god-component
findings.detail: One file holds DayView, WeekView, MonthView, QuarterView, RoundsView, JobDrawer, NewJobModal, MobileDayStrip, FilterBar, DateNav, ViewTabs, SortableJobCard, JobBlock, HourRuler, CrewLane, StatStrip, demo data, theme constants, layout engine, and the top-level container. NewJobModal alone (lines 1635-2017) is ~380 lines. JobDrawer (1385-1563) ~180 lines. Cognitive load is enormous, file is impossible to test, hot-reload is slow.
findings.fix: Split into src/pages/scheduler/ with files: Scheduler.jsx (root only), views/{DayView,WeekView,MonthView,QuarterView,RoundsView}.jsx, components/{JobDrawer,NewJobModal,FilterBar,DateNav,ViewTabs,MobileDayStrip,StatStrip,JobBlock,CrewLane}.jsx, lib/{theme.js,dates.js,layoutDayJobs.js,demoData.js}. Extract shared constants (TYPE, STATUS_STYLES, STATUS_LABELS) to src/lib/jobTheme.js so RoutePlanner + SchedulerPreview can import them.

findings.title: TYPE / colour constants duplicated across 3 files
findings.detail: Confirmed. Scheduler.jsx has full TYPE object with 4 keys (incl. site_visit). SchedulerPreview has identical 3-key version. RoutePlanner has TYPE_DOT + TYPE_CHIP separately. Drift risk is real — Scheduler has a 4th key (site_visit) the others don't.
findings.fix: Create src/lib/jobTheme.js exporting TYPE, STATUS_STYLES, STATUS_LABELS, tintForCrew, CREW_PALETTE. All 3 files import from there. Adds a single source of truth and lets a future Site Visit type flow through to RoutePlanner.

findings.title: DayView and WeekView duplicate filter logic verbatim
findings.detail: Confirmed. Both compute the same `if (typeFilter !== 'all') ... if (crewFilter !== 'all') ...` block including the unused '__unassigned__' branch. MonthView and QuarterView don't apply these filters at all, which is itself a UX inconsistency.
findings.fix: Extract `function applyJobFilters(jobs, { typeFilter, crewFilter })` in scheduler/lib/filters.js. Apply consistently in all 4 views so the type/crew filter strip means the same thing in every view.

findings.title: Time-formatting helpers duplicated, hourToTime redefined inside NewJobModal
findings.detail: fmtTime + fmtTime12 are top-level (Scheduler.jsx 248), but NewJobModal redefines hourToTime locally (1639). RoutePlanner has no 12-hour formatter at all but uses its own fmt2/fmtMi/fmtDate set.
findings.fix: Move all time/money formatters into src/lib/format.js (fmtMoney, fmtTime, fmtTime12, hourToTime, durLabel). NewJobModal imports hourToTime instead of redefining it.

findings.title: Modal shell template repeated across 3 modals
findings.detail: Confirmed. Same fixed-inset overlay + GCard/glass header + close-X + footer button row. RoutePlanner uses dark glass; Scheduler uses light glass — but the structural skeleton is the same.
findings.fix: Create <ModalShell title, subtitle, onClose, footer, theme='light'|'dark'> in src/components/Modal.jsx. All three modals reduce to body content only.

findings.title: Rounds 'Schedule' button is a dead-end — only pre-fills customer name field
findings.detail: Confirmed. onScheduleRound just runs `setPreCustomer(round.roundName); setShowNewJob(true)`. The whole round (10–20 customers, postcodes, prices, recurrence, due dates) is collapsed to one string in the customer field of a single empty job. There is no batch creation of jobs for the round's customers, no auto-assign, no recurrence carry-through. Users will tap it expecting 'schedule the round' and get one job titled 'Monday AM — Kensington'.
findings.fix: Either (a) wire a real batch flow: open a 'Schedule round' modal that lists each customer with editable date/time/assignee then creates N jobs; or (b) rename the button 'New job from round' and leave a TODO. Until (a) lands, hiding the button on live data (it's most dangerous when dbRounds has real customers) is the safer interim.

findings.title: WeekView assumes j.day is set; only safe because parent decorates it
findings.detail: Verified. WeekView reads `j.day` and stuffs into map[0..6]. j.day is added by parent in weekJobsWithDay (line 2283). If WeekView is ever rendered with raw weekJobs by accident, every job is silently dropped (the `if (j.day >= 0 && j.day < 7)` filters undefined to false). No warning, no fallback.
findings.fix: Either compute day-of-week inside WeekView from j.date (decouples view from parent), or warn/coerce in WeekView: if j.day == null, derive from j.date. Drop the parent's weekJobsWithDay decorator.

findings.title: crewFilter '__unassigned__' branch is unreachable
findings.detail: Confirmed dead code. deriveCrews (line 524-550) DOES emit an '__unassigned__' crew id when jobs are unassigned, AND sorts it last — so the branch IS reachable when there are unassigned jobs. Mapper claim is wrong. However: the FilterBar (line 428-451) only renders these crews when `crews.length > 0` and renders each crew's name including 'Unassigned'. Verify: it does render. So this branch is live. Mark mapper finding as false.
findings.fix: No change. Mapper was wrong — the '__unassigned__' branch is reachable through deriveCrews.

findings.title: Month/Quarter views are read-only, lack edit affordance
findings.detail: Confirmed. MonthView expanded-day list calls onJobClick (which opens the drawer — actually editable). QuarterView has no job click at all. So Month is partially actionable (via drawer), Quarter is fully read-only. That's defensible for a summary view but users may tap the bars expecting drill-down.
findings.fix: In QuarterView, make week bars clickable to switch view to Week with that offset. Not really a 'dead-end' for Month since the drawer opens via the expanded list.

findings.title: Mobile search bar is missing (hidden md:block on desktop input)
findings.detail: Confirmed. The search input wraps in `<div className='relative hidden md:block'>`. There's no mobile equivalent — search state still exists but cannot be set on mobile. All view filtering on `search` therefore can't be exercised by phone users.
findings.fix: Either show a compact search icon-button on mobile that toggles a full-width search input below the StatStrip, or move the search field below the header on mobile (sm:hidden block).

findings.title: NewJobModal initial-state useEffect fetches team_members on every mount with no cleanup
findings.detail: Each time the modal opens, it dynamic-imports supabase and queries team_members. Errors are silently swallowed (.catch(()=>{}) both at the import and the query level). No cleanup if user closes the modal mid-fetch — setStaffOptions can fire on an unmounted component (React 18 tolerates it but it's a smell). On a slow connection, the modal opens with just 'You/Unassigned' and silently 'upgrades' later — confusing if you've already chosen.
findings.fix: Lift team_members fetch into DataContext (it's needed elsewhere too — Rota/Pay/Holiday). Read from context. If you must keep it local, add a cancelled flag in cleanup and surface fetch errors with a small inline notice.

findings.title: NewJobModal recurring expansion: hard-coded 11 future occurrences regardless of cadence
findings.detail: `for (let i = 0; i < 11; i++)` creates 11 future jobs no matter what — for a daily round that's only 11 days of work; for an 8-weekly round it's ~22 months ahead. Each call uses `id: Date.now() + i + 1` which can collide if two recurring jobs are created in the same ms across users. Also onSave is called 12 times synchronously — if onSave optimistically inserts into DataContext, that's 12 re-renders, and if any insert fails partway you get a partial recurring series with no rollback.
findings.fix: Replace client-side expansion with a server-side recurrence column on jobs + a daily edge function that materialises N days ahead. Or at minimum: cap by date (12 months out), not by count; use crypto.randomUUID() for ids; batch the inserts via Promise.allSettled and roll back if any fail.

findings.title: DayView initialOrder useMemo dep uses joined string — fragile invalidation
findings.detail: `useMemo(..., [filteredJobs.map(j => j.id + j.status).join(',')])` works but disables exhaustive-deps and is O(n) string-build every render. The follow-up useEffect resyncs `order` from `initialOrder` whenever the join changes — meaning any status flip wipes the user's drag order. Combined with the drag-end handler that mutates start_hour by 0.01 to 'persist' order (line 833-836), this is a hidden race: status changes → effect resets order → drag offsets are lost until the next status change.
findings.fix: Use a stable dep like `filteredJobs.length + ':' + completedCount + ':' + dayOffset`. Better: persist a true `display_order` column on jobs rather than abusing start_hour. The start_hour micro-spread (baseHour + i*0.01) is misleading data — a job 'scheduled for 08:01' is wrong if base was 08:00 and it's actually the 60th in line.

findings.title: RoundsView swallows listAllRounds error and falls back to demo data
findings.detail: `listAllRounds().then(setDbRounds).catch(() => setDbRounds([]))`. On error, dbRounds is set to []. Then `isDemo = !dbRounds || dbRounds.length === 0` — so a failed fetch silently shows demo data with a 'Demo data shown' banner, indistinguishable from a fresh account with no rounds. Users with real rounds will see DEMO_ROUNDS (Kensington, West Nine) appear in their tab on a transient network error. Confusing and looks like data loss.
findings.fix: Three states: loading (null), error (Error), empty ([]), populated. Show distinct UI: spinner / 'Couldn't load rounds — retry' / current 'Import rounds' empty state / list. Don't conflate error with empty.

findings.title: JobDrawer save/delete handlers swallow error details
findings.detail: `} catch { setDrawerError('Could not save changes. Please try again.'); }` discards the error. No telemetry, no console. If onUpdateJob rejects because of e.g. a RLS policy violation or constraint error, the user just sees 'Please try again' forever.
findings.fix: `} catch (err) { console.error('[JobDrawer] save failed', err); setDrawerError(err?.message || 'Could not save…'); }`. Same for handleDelete. Consider a global toast/log hook.

findings.title: RoutePlanner SaveRoute and DeleteRoute optimistic updates on failure silently desync
findings.detail: handleDeleteRoute removes from local state first, then tries deleteRoute(id) — on failure shows a 5s toast that 'will reappear on next load'. But the local state already removed it; user thinks it's gone. handleSaveRoute on createRoute failure inserts a local-only entry with a fake id `r${Date.now()}` and a toast — that entry then won't sync, won't update, won't delete cleanly later (deleteRoute(localId) will 404 silently). Mileage log path has the same flaw (line 832-836).
findings.fix: For delete: try server first, then remove on success — or roll back local removal on failure. For save: don't insert a fake-id row; show retry. Add a 'pending sync' badge if you want to keep optimistic UX.

findings.title: RoutePlanner AddStopModal cannot be dismissed by overlay click or Esc
findings.detail: Verified. The outer overlay div has no onClick={onClose}. Same for SaveRouteModal (line 412). Only the explicit ✕ button dismisses. Compare with Scheduler.jsx JobDrawer (line 1420) which does have overlay onClick. Inconsistent UX; accessibility miss (no Esc handler anywhere).
findings.fix: Add onClick={onClose} to overlay + e.stopPropagation on inner; add useEffect to listen for Escape. Bake into the ModalShell extraction above.

findings.title: RoutePlanner Optimise button uses inline 25-line nearest-neighbour algorithm
findings.detail: Inline anonymous function inside JSX onClick. Untestable, untyped. Also: starts from prev[0] (the first stop the user added) rather than from homePostcode, so 'optimal' may start at the furthest stop.
findings.fix: Extract `function nearestNeighbourOrder(stops, homePostcode)` to lib/route.js. Start from home so the first leg is also optimised. Unit test it.

findings.title: Recurrence weekly arithmetic uses 30/91 days (calendar drift)
findings.detail: `monthly: 30, quarterly: 91` — over 12 occurrences that's 60+ days of drift; a monthly clean booked for the 1st will land on the 31st-ish of month 11. Users will not notice until the calendar shows the wrong date.
findings.fix: Use real calendar arithmetic: setMonth(+1) for monthly, setMonth(+3) for quarterly. Keep day-of-month constant with clamp for short months. The +14, +7, +42, etc. weekly variants are fine.

findings.title: weekJobsWithDay rebuilds on every weekJobs reference change but isn't memoised against contents
findings.detail: weekJobs is recomputed when allJobs identity changes (DataContext refetch). weekJobsWithDay then maps it. Each render in Week view re-instantiates the array. Then WeekView's `useMemo(jobsByDay, [filteredJobs])` triggers because filteredJobs depends on jobs reference. Not catastrophic but the entire chain rebuilds on every parent render of the StatStrip count-up animation.
findings.fix: Stabilise allJobs in DataContext (return same reference if no change), or merge the .map(day) step into the weekJobs useMemo so there's one fewer link in the chain.

findings.title: Search filter uses outdated `searchLower` captured outside useMemo deps
findings.detail: `const searchLower = search.toLowerCase()` is computed at the top of every render, then several useMemos reference `searchLower` but only declare `search` in their dep list (with eslint-disable). It works because `search` changes ⇒ render ⇒ new searchLower ⇒ new useMemo. But the eslint-disable hides that monthJobs has `[allJobs, viewDate, search]` while quarterJobs deps don't include search (line 2304-2312) — meaning Quarter view doesn't react to search. Cross-view inconsistency.
findings.fix: Either apply search uniformly across all views' useMemos, or strip search from all of them and apply it once at headerJobs derivation. Drop the eslint-disable lines and let the linter catch missing deps.

findings.title: isLive check duplicated and inconsistent
findings.detail: Scheduler: `isLive = Boolean(user) && !isDemoUser` (user id 'demo-user' is treated as not-live). RoutePlanner: `isLive = Boolean(user)` (treats demo-user as live → would call real Supabase). If demo-user has a session, RoutePlanner will hit Supabase under demo creds.
findings.fix: Move isDemoUser/isLive into AuthContext so all tabs agree. RoutePlanner currently inserts real routes/mileage for the demo user account.

findings.title: team_members RLS policy missing WITH CHECK clause
findings.detail: `CREATE POLICY "team_members_owner" ON public.team_members FOR ALL USING (business_id = auth.uid());` has no WITH CHECK. With FOR ALL and no WITH CHECK the USING clause is reused for writes, but the policy itself does not constrain INSERT/UPDATE of `business_id` against another user's value. Combined with the fact that `business_id` here references `profiles(id)` and is treated as `auth.uid()` (not the canonical `my_business_id()` model used by jobs/customer_rounds), an authenticated user can INSERT a team_member with their own auth.uid() — fine — but the inconsistency means non-owner staff in a multi-user business literally cannot see ANY team_members (RLS forbids it), which silently breaks the Scheduler's staff dropdown for Britannia's 1500-staff org.
findings.fix: Rewrite to `business_id = my_business_id()` (or add a membership table check) with explicit `WITH CHECK (business_id = my_business_id())` to match the model used for jobs, customer_rounds, customers. Migrate existing rows.

findings.title: No role-based RLS — staff can read every customer's address and price across the whole business
findings.detail: Jobs/customers/customer_rounds policies are flat `business_id = my_business_id()` — any authenticated user in the business gets full CRUD on every job (price, notes, address, postcode) and every customer record. There is no `staff vs owner` differentiation. For Britannia's 1500-employee deployment this means any cleaner can read the entire customer book, mark any job complete, change prices, or delete jobs. UK-GDPR Article 5(1)(f) (integrity & confidentiality) and Article 32 (appropriate access control) require least-privilege.
findings.fix: Add a role column to a membership table and split policies: `jobs_staff_read` limited to rows where `assignee_ids @> array[auth.uid()]`; `jobs_owner_all` for owners/managers. Same for customers (staff sees only customers they have an assigned job for).

findings.title: Duplicate/competing jobs table definition and RLS model
findings.detail: 003_missing_tables.sql creates `jobs` with policy `jobs_owner_all` keyed on `owner_id = auth.uid()`. 007_cadi_foundations.sql also creates `jobs` (`create table if not exists`) with policy `jobs_business_all` keyed on `business_id = my_business_id()`. Postgres RLS combines multiple permissive policies with OR — if both survived the migration history, ANY row owned by the user OR in their business is visible, and the schema becomes ambiguous. If 003 ran first and the policy was never dropped, owners may still hold rows via owner_id that bypass `business_id` checks.
findings.fix: Audit live DB with `select policyname, cmd, qual, with_check from pg_policies where tablename='jobs'`. Drop the stale `jobs_owner_all` policy explicitly and document the canonical RLS in a new migration.

findings.title: Customer PII exfiltrated to Google Maps without consent
findings.detail: `window.open(https://www.google.com/maps/dir/${postcodes.join('/')}, ...)` and the equivalent `<a href={googleMapsUrl}>` in RoutePlanner.jsx:877,1009,1014 send every customer's postcode (often pinning to a single residential property in low-density areas) to Google. The Referer header also leaks the originating Cadi URL, which on the New Job flow contains `?customer=<NAME>` (line 2225). This is a cross-border (UK→US) transfer of personal data with no DPA, no consent UI, and no record in the privacy notice. UK-GDPR Articles 13, 28, 44 are engaged.
findings.fix: (a) Add a consent toggle in business settings before enabling external map links; (b) set `referrerPolicy="no-referrer"` on the <a> and use noopener; (c) document Google as a sub-processor in the privacy notice; (d) consider an in-app map using a UK/EU provider (Ordnance Survey, HERE EU) for default.

findings.title: Customer name placed in URL query string
findings.detail: `searchParams.get('customer')` — the customer name is passed via `?customer=NAME` to pre-fill the New Job modal. URL params land in browser history, server access logs, and the Referer header on any subsequent navigation (including the Google Maps click immediately above). Customer name is personal data under UK-GDPR.
findings.fix: Pass the prefill via router state (`navigate(path, { state: { customer } })`) instead of query string; or clear the param synchronously before any external link can be clicked.

findings.title: Hardcoded postcode-to-coordinate table silently falls back to London centroid — bad HMRC mileage records
findings.detail: `getCoords` returns `[51.505,-0.09]` (London centroid) for any postcode not in the hardcoded SW/SE/W/EC/WC/N/NW/E table. Distances are then computed against that centroid and written to `mileage_logs` as the user's HMRC claim. Users outside central London will submit materially incorrect mileage to HMRC (offence under HMRC record-keeping rules; also misleads the user). The on-screen disclaimer at line 609 is buried.
findings.fix: Use a real postcode lookup (postcodes.io is free and UK-hosted) and refuse to log mileage when fallback is used. Surface a per-leg warning if a postcode was not resolved.

findings.title: Drag-to-reorder overwrites real start_hour times with fake 0.01h increments
findings.detail: `updateJob(id, { start_hour: baseHour + i * 0.01 })` persists synthetic times (gaps of <1 minute) when a user drags to reorder. Real scheduled appointment times are destroyed in the DB. Not a security bug but corrupts the business record — relevant for VAT/MTD evidence and customer dispute resolution.
findings.fix: Add a separate `sort_order` integer column for drag-order; never mutate `start_hour` from a drag.

findings.title: Mileage_logs and saved_routes are owner-only; non-owner staff invisible
findings.detail: `saved_routes_owner_all` and `mileage_logs_owner_all` both gate on `owner_id = auth.uid()`. In a multi-driver business (Britannia: many vehicles) only the owner can see their own routes/mileage; other drivers will see empty UI with no error. This is inconsistent with the business_id model used for jobs and is a silent data-isolation failure (drivers may double-log mileage). Also: when a staff member is removed, ON DELETE CASCADE on auth.users would wipe their HMRC mileage records — HMRC requires retention for 6 years after the tax year.
findings.fix: Switch to business_id model, add a `driver_user_id` column, and replace cascade with `SET NULL` for the mileage_logs FK so records survive staff deletion.

findings.title: No data subject access / erasure path from Schedule tab
findings.detail: UK-GDPR Articles 15 (access) and 17 (erasure) require the data controller to action subject requests. Customers are stored across jobs, customer_rounds, customers, and free-text `notes` fields on jobs. There is no admin tool to (a) export everything tied to a customer or (b) hard-delete or anonymise on request. Soft-delete only via `status='cancelled'` leaves PII (name, address, postcode, notes) indefinitely.
findings.fix: Add a customer-level 'Export & forget' action that returns JSON of all jobs/rounds/notes and either anonymises PII (`name='[redacted]'`, addresses null) or hard-deletes after retaining the legally required fields for tax/HMRC. Log every export to an audit table.

findings.title: No audit trail of who viewed / edited customer or job records
findings.detail: `jobs` and `customers` have no row-level audit log. For 1500-staff Britannia deployment, GDPR Article 32 (security) and the ICO's expectations on accountability require knowing who accessed customer PII and price data. Without this, insider misuse (cleaner sells customer list) cannot be detected or evidenced.
findings.fix: Add an `access_log` table written via a trigger or via the existing `job_events` table; record SELECT-after-search at the app layer for customer lookup (Scheduler.jsx:1878).

findings.title: team_members table contains staff PII (email, phone) under permissive RLS
findings.detail: team_members stores `email` and `phone` (staff personal data) but the RLS policy is FOR ALL — meaning every authenticated user matching `business_id = auth.uid()` can read every colleague's contact details. In Britannia this is fine for the owner but excessive once staff log in (no read-only / no-PII-projection split).
findings.fix: Create a SECURITY DEFINER view `team_members_public` exposing only id, first_name, last_name, is_active, and grant SELECT on that to non-owner roles. Reserve direct SELECT on team_members for owners/managers.

findings.title: No XSS or SQL-injection vectors found
findings.detail: All user-supplied strings (customer, notes, service) are rendered via JSX text interpolation — no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no innerHTML, no SQL string interpolation. Supabase client uses parameterised queries throughout. Search and prefill are kept client-side.
findings.fix: No action needed. Keep current pattern; add ESLint rule `react/no-danger` to prevent regressions.

findings.title: Natural-language schedule editor ("Move Mrs Patel to Thursday and swap crew B with crew C on Tuesday")
findings.detail: Trigger: a chat input pinned to the Schedule header (or AskCadi already mounted) accepting free-text commands like 'shift everything on Friday by 30 mins, swap Jake and Mel after 1pm, and slot the new Britannia office visit somewhere sensible Wednesday morning'. Claude (Sonnet) receives the current day/week jobs JSON, crew roster, and customer access constraints; outputs a structured JSON patch (array of {jobId, changes:{date,start_hour,crewId,staffIds}}) plus a human summary of conflicts ('Mrs Patel only allows Tues/Thurs — kept her on Thursday'). UI shows a diff preview with Apply/Undo. Wow factor: every competitor (Squeegee, Aworka, Jobber) forces drag-drop one job at a time; Cadi takes a sentence and rewrites the week. Data deps: jobs, customers (access windows, preferred days), staff, crews. Model: Sonnet (multi-constraint reasoning). Compounds with Customers tab (access prefs) and Staff tab (availability/holiday).
findings.fix: Add NLScheduleCommand component above day grid, post {prompt, scheduleSnapshot, constraints} to edge function ai-schedule-edit, return JSON patch, render ScheduleDiffModal with Apply button calling updateJob in batch.

findings.title: Auto-fill empty slots from the unassigned/round backlog
findings.detail: Trigger: 'Fill day' button on Day view when a crew has >60 min of gap, or a banner 'Crew B has 2h spare — auto-fill?'. Claude (Sonnet) receives gap windows per crew, the rounds backlog (next-due recurring customers), postcodes for current jobs adjacent to the gap, and crew skill tags. Outputs a ranked list of suggested fills with reasoning ('Mr Davies is due tomorrow but lives 4 min from Crew B's 11:00 stop — pull him forward'). User clicks Apply All or cherry-picks. Wow factor: turns dead time into revenue automatically — Pinnacle/Deputy can't do this because they don't know customer geography or due dates. Data deps: jobs.start_hour/duration, rounds (next_due), customers.postcode, crews. Model: Sonnet. Compounds with Rounds + Customers + Route Planner.
findings.fix: ai-schedule-fill edge function returning [{jobCandidate, reason, confidence}], render as suggestion cards in the gap on day grid.

findings.title: Conflict & risk detector with proactive nudges
findings.detail: Trigger: passive — runs on every job mutation; surfaces as a small amber chip on day/week view ('3 risks — review'). Claude (Haiku, cheap) ingests the day's schedule + staff holiday + customer notes ('do not arrive before 10am', 'dog in garden Tuesdays') + weather (exterior jobs) and emits a list {jobId, riskType, severity, suggestedFix}. Examples: 'Window clean at 14 Park Rd scheduled in 80% rain probability', 'Jake is on holiday Thursday but assigned to 3 jobs', 'Two jobs overlap on Crew C 11:00–11:40'. Wow factor: catches the £200 mistake before it happens — no competitor cross-checks weather + holidays + customer notes. Data deps: jobs, staff.holiday/absence, customers.notes, weather API, hr_settings. Model: Haiku. Compounds with Staff tab (holiday engine already built) and Customers.
findings.fix: ai-schedule-risks edge function on cron + on-write trigger; render ScheduleRiskPanel that opens drawer to fix each.

findings.title: Smart recurrence inference from a single job
findings.detail: Trigger: when creating a new job in the modal, after entering customer + service, an inline AI suggestion: 'Looks like a 4-weekly clean — based on similar £45 residential jobs in your book they usually recur every 28 days on the same weekday. Apply?'. Claude (Haiku) gets {newJob, last 50 jobs for similar service/price band, customer history if existing}, returns {recurrence: '4-weekly', dayOfWeek: 'Tue', confidence, reasoning}. Wow factor: removes the most common data-entry mistake (one-off vs recurring) — Squeegee makes you tick boxes manually. Data deps: jobs history, services, customers. Model: Haiku. Compounds with Services + Customers.
findings.fix: Add useEffect in job-create modal calling ai-recurrence-suggest after customer+service selected; show pill above recurrence dropdown.

findings.title: Route-optimised day re-sequencer (one-tap)
findings.detail: Trigger: 'Optimise day' button on the Day view per crew, and a 'Optimise all crews' on the stat strip. Claude (Sonnet) gets the crew's jobs with postcodes, durations, customer access windows, and home_postcode start point. Outputs a re-ordered start_hour array plus expected mileage saved and time saved ('save 38 min and 14 miles — Mrs Patel still inside 10–12 window'). Wow factor: Jobber's route optimiser is bolt-on $20/mo and ignores access windows; Cadi does it inline, respects constraints, and explains the trade-offs in English. Data deps: jobs, customers (access windows), profiles.home_postcode, plus Google Maps distance matrix. Model: Sonnet (for explaining trade-offs); could use OR-tools deterministic + Claude for narration. Compounds with Route Planner tab.
findings.fix: ai-optimise-route edge function; render OptimisePreviewModal with before/after timeline; Apply calls batch updateJob.

findings.title: Voice/text capture: 'Add Mrs Henderson 2pm Friday, regular conservatory, £35'
findings.detail: Trigger: floating mic + text bar 'Quick add' on Day/Week views. Claude (Haiku) parses one-line natural input into {customer (match existing or create), service (match from services list), date, start_hour, duration, price, recurrence}. Shows confirm card ('Found Mrs Henderson at 22 Oak Lane — use her?'). Wow factor: at scale (Britannia 1500 staff, hundreds of admins) saving 30 seconds per job is huge; voice means it works while driving between jobs. Data deps: customers, services. Model: Haiku. Compounds with ServiceChat parser (already Haiku-ready) and CustomerImport.
findings.fix: QuickAddBar component → ai-parse-job edge fn → addJobAndSyncCustomer.

findings.title: Weekly pre-brief generator for crews ('Tomorrow's run-sheet')
findings.detail: Trigger: 'Send brief' button on Day view (also auto-fires at 6pm via cron). For each crew, Claude (Haiku) generates a plain-English SMS/email/push: 'Hi Jake — 7 jobs tomorrow, start 8:30 at Mrs Patel. Watch out: 11 Oak Lane wants side gate not front, Mrs Davies cancelled Friday so swap to Mr Singh. Total 6.5h, 22 miles.' Wow factor: BrightHR sends generic shift reminders; this synthesises customer notes + route + changes since last visit. Data deps: jobs, customers.notes, staff contacts, change log. Model: Haiku. Compounds with Staff tab (My Pay/My Schedule in StaffDashboard).
findings.fix: ai-crew-brief edge fn called from button + scheduled-tasks daily 18:00; deliver via existing notification rails.

findings.title: Revenue gap analyser on Quarter view
findings.detail: Trigger: 'Why is week 7 dipping?' button on the 13-week revenue bar chart, plus passive insight chips beneath each bar. Claude (Sonnet) receives the 13 weeks of jobs/revenue/completion + historical baseline + bank holidays + staff absence + cancellation reasons. Outputs 3-bullet insight: 'Week 7 down £620 because: (1) 2 commercial jobs deferred to wk 8 by Britannia, (2) Crew C lost 1.5 days holiday, (3) 4 residential cancellations clustered on Tuesday — weather (storm Henk).' Wow factor: turns the chart from vanity metric into a coachable answer — no scheduler competitor does causal analysis. Data deps: jobs, cancellations, staff.absence, weather history. Model: Sonnet. Compounds with Payroll (labour cost) and Quarter reporting.
findings.fix: ai-quarter-insights edge fn → InsightCard under the bar chart on click.

findings.title: Customer-side reschedule negotiator (auto-replies)
findings.detail: Trigger: a customer texts/emails 'Can we move next Tuesday?'. Inbox chip on Schedule says '3 reschedule requests'. Claude (Sonnet) reads the message, the customer's preferences, current crew availability for the next 14 days, and proposes 2–3 alternative slots that respect crew route logic and customer's historical day-of-week pattern. Drafts the reply for one-click send. Wow factor: this is the single biggest admin time-sink for cleaning firms and nobody automates it well. Data deps: inbox/email integration, jobs, customers, crews. Model: Sonnet. Compounds with Front Desk + Customers + Schedule.
findings.fix: ai-reschedule-negotiator edge fn + new RescheduleInbox panel + email/SMS webhook ingestion.

findings.title: End-of-day completion summariser + invoice trigger
findings.detail: Trigger: when last job of the day flips to 'complete', a modal asks 'Wrap up Tuesday?'. Claude (Haiku) summarises the day: completed/missed/notes-added jobs, total revenue, mileage, hours per crew, customers needing invoice/follow-up, and drafts the entries for RoutePlanner mileage log and any 'follow-up needed' tasks. One click pushes invoices to billing queue and mileage to the log. Wow factor: collapses 20 minutes of admin into one confirm — competitors leave each step in a separate screen. Data deps: jobs, RoutePlanner mileage rows, invoicing queue. Model: Haiku. Compounds with RoutePlanner, Invoices, Customers.
findings.fix: Add EodWrapUpModal triggered on last-complete; ai-eod-summary edge fn; Apply calls batch inserts to mileage_log + invoices_queue.

findings.title: Dead 'Call' and 'Add note' quick actions sit alongside working buttons
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1761-1766 — In the Overview tab's 2x2 quick action grid, the 'Call' and 'Add note' buttons render identically to the working 'Send message' / 'Book job' buttons but have no onClick handler. A real cleaner standing in the van will tap Call expecting tel: to fire. Silence breaks trust on first contact.
findings.fix: Wire Call to `tel:${customer.phone}` (with disabled state + 'No phone on file' affordance matching the email pattern in MessageComposer at 913-921). Wire Add note to open an inline note appender that prepends timestamped lines to customer.notes — bonus: voice-to-text via the Web Speech API would be a genuine 'wow' for cleaners with wet/gloved hands.

findings.title: 'Customer portal' teaser appears in two places with no path forward
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1518-1527 shows a 'Coming soon' chip on the profile header, and Customers.jsx:706-708 shows a 'Portal ready' dot on every large-density card. Both are dead. The 'Portal ready' label actively misleads — it implies a state the user can act on. On first use a cleaner will hover, click, and find nothing.
findings.fix: Either (a) hide entirely until the portal ships behind a feature flag, or (b) convert to a waitlist CTA ('Be first to try it') that captures intent so you have a launch list. Drop 'Portal ready' from the card — it's noise without action.

findings.title: Hardcoded staff PINs in source code — security illusion, not security
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:945-950 defines VAULT_STAFF with plaintext PINs '1234' / '5678' / '0000'. Every user of Cadi shares these PINs. The vault writes both data and the access log to localStorage (Customers.jsx:1014, 965-967), so it's per-device only — staff using the iPad won't see what the manager logged on the laptop. Britannia-scale (1500 staff) cannot use this at all. The visual promise (lock icon, 4-digit pad, auto-lock countdown, access log) sets a security expectation the implementation doesn't keep.
findings.fix: Move PINs to a Supabase `staff` table tied to owner_id; verify server-side via edge function; store vault contents encrypted in DB so they sync across devices and survive a wiped browser. Until then, label the vault honestly ('Quick-access codes, this device only') so a manager doesn't assume it's audited.

findings.title: Vault unlocks for ANY valid PIN — no identity, log is anonymous-by-design
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1001-1024 — verifyPin matches the entered code against the staff array and logs whoever owns that PIN. There's no 'who is using this device' step. If Tom borrows Emma's phone and types his PIN, the log is correct; if anyone reads Emma's PIN off a sticker by the van keys, the log records Emma. The 30s auto-lock + countdown bar (1178-1188) is a beautiful micro-interaction wrapped around an essentially shared secret.
findings.fix: Pair PIN with a staff selector ('Who's looking?') before showing the keypad, OR move to per-staff magic-link auth so the PIN is just a re-auth prompt. Show the staff name on the unlock bar (currently just '🔓 Unlocked' at 1170-1174) so social-pressure deters PIN sharing.

findings.title: AI suggestions hide the most valuable insight — the £ on the table
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1884-1915 renders suggestions as priority-tagged cards inside the customer detail. The cards show +£amount per suggestion (1902) but the LIST itself surfaces only '💡 {title}' in tiny amber text (686, 625). A cleaner scanning 80 customers cannot see which 5 are worth £1k+ in unbooked upsells. This is Cadi's most defensible feature and it's whispered.
findings.fix: On the list row (large + medium density), replace 'Portal ready' with the highest-value suggestion's £ figure as a chip: '£250 opportunity'. Add a dashboard-style banner above the list: 'You have £840 in suggested upsells across 12 customers — review →' that filters to sortBy='urgent'. This is the daily-open hook.

findings.title: Empty 'Message history' is a missed loop — messages sent never come back
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1942-1948 hardcodes a placeholder 'No messages logged yet. Send your first message above — it will appear here.' But MessageComposer at 911-921 only opens a `mailto:` link or copies to clipboard — nothing is persisted. The promise is broken on first send. Compare to Jobber/ServiceM8 where every comms touchpoint is timeline'd.
findings.fix: When 'Send email' or 'Copy text' fires (912-927), insert a row into a `customer_messages` table with type/subject/body/timestamp. Show them as a timeline. For real wow: detect Gmail/Outlook replies via SES/forwarding so the conversation threads automatically.

findings.title: Sort/filter UI is dense and uses three competing patterns
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:2463-2613 — within ~150 lines the header stacks: segment pill-tabs (2464-2484), status filter chips (2487-2501), search (2540-2568), horizontal job-type chip scroller (2571-2587), and two native <select> dropdowns for status+sort (2590-2611). Status appears TWICE (chips and select). On a phone this is a wall of controls before you see a single customer. The pretty design has buried the table.
findings.fix: Collapse into one Filter pill that opens a sheet (segment + status + type + sort in one place), with active filters shown as removable chips inline with the search bar. Surface only sort (with sensible default = 'Urgent first') and the search bar by default. Remove the duplicate status select.

findings.title: Default sort is alphabetical — buries the money
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:2309 initialises `sortBy='name'`. The urgent-scored sort (2381-2389) exists and is the killer view — but you must discover it via a dropdown labelled 'Sort: Urgent first'. A cleaner opens the tab and sees Aaron Adams at the top instead of 'Margaret — £250 roof treatment due'.
findings.fix: Default to 'urgent' if any customer has urgent/high suggestions, else 'recent'. Save the user's last choice. This is one line of code that changes what Cadi feels like.

findings.title: GoCardless card always rendered even with zero context for new users
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1957-2038 — the Direct Debit card appears on EVERY customer detail regardless of whether the user has connected GoCardless at the account level. A free-tier owner who's never heard of GoCardless sees a 'Set up GoCardless →' button, clicks, and the edge function (1395-1408) will fail with an opaque error. There's no explainer of what GoCardless IS, what it costs, or why a UK cleaner would want it (vs SumUp / Stripe / bank transfer).
findings.fix: Gate the card behind a 'GoCardless connected' account flag. For unconnected users show a one-time educational card: 'Get paid automatically — £0.20 + 1% per Direct Debit, no chasing'. The wow moment is the FIRST payment auto-collecting — surface a notification when the first £ lands.

findings.title: Star rating in header is a tap-trap — easy to set 1-star by accident
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1483-1485 puts a 5-star clickable rating directly under the customer name in the header. There's no confirm, no undo toast, and onChange writes immediately to the DB (StarRating at 505-513 → onUpdateCustomer). A scrolling finger on mobile WILL hit a star. Worse, the rating is visible on every list row when set (693-697), so it looks like a customer rating of YOU.
findings.fix: Move to the Overview body section, label it 'Your private rating of this customer' (it currently has zero label — Customers.jsx:1484), and add an undo toast when changed. Or make it click-to-edit instead of one-tap.

findings.title: Add Customer modal lacks the highest-leverage field: 'How did they find you?'
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:2260-2263 has a free-text 'Source' input ('Referral, leaflet…'). Free text means you can never aggregate. A 5-second dropdown ('Referral / Google / Checkatrade / Facebook / Leaflet / Repeat / Walk-by / Other') would unlock a Marketing tab later that says 'Your last 30 customers came from: Checkatrade 12, Referral 9...'. Genuine cleaner gold.
findings.fix: Convert Source to a select + 'Add custom'. Then add a stat to the header strip (2520-2537): 'Top source this month: Referrals (9)'. That's the wow moment — Cadi tells them where their growth actually comes from.

findings.title: AddCustomer modal misses inline postcode → address lookup
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:2208-2230 — five separate address inputs (line1/line2/town/county/postcode). UK cleaners are used to Getaddress.io / Postcoder where typing a postcode autofills the rest. Compared to Squeegee / CleanerPlanner this feels old-school. First-time experience: typing 'Chestnut Cottage, Long Sutton Road, Long Sutton, Lincolnshire, PE12 9AB' five times in a day.
findings.fix: Add a postcode-first lookup at the top of the address section ('Enter postcode → pick address'). Use a free tier of getaddress.io or ideal-postcodes. This is THE delight moment for UK SMB onboarding — competitors don't all do it.

findings.title: Empty state for a fresh account is bland — wastes the moment of arrival
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:2658-2679 — 'Add your first customer' with a 👥 emoji, two buttons. A cleaner who's just signed up to Cadi after seeing the marketing site is at peak attention here. Compare to Linear's empty states which show a sample card. Cadi could offer 'Try with sample data (Margaret, Greenfield, David) →' so the user sees suggestions / vault / messaging working in 5 seconds.
findings.fix: Add a third button: 'See how it works with sample customers'. Seed 5-10 demo customers tagged demo=true (the DEMO_CUSTOMERS array at lines 234-381 is still in the file as a comment — resurrect it). Add a one-click 'Clear sample data' when they're ready.

findings.title: Import wizard celebrates loudly but the user lands on a list they don't understand
findings.detail: CustomerImport.jsx:930-960 — the StepDone screen has confetti, count-up animation, 24 confetti pieces. This is the strongest 'wow' in the whole tab. But once dismissed (Customers.jsx:2766-2778) the user lands on a now-densely-populated list with no orientation. The work of teaching them the new view (segments / suggestions / urgency) is dropped at the highest-attention moment.
findings.fix: After import, auto-select sortBy='urgent' so the highest-opportunity customer is the first row. Add a one-time coachmark on the first suggestion card: 'Margaret has a £250 roof treatment due — tap to draft the message'. Convert confetti energy into the first money-making action.

findings.title: Suggestions never disappear — no way to dismiss or snooze
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:52-193 generateSuggestions is pure-functional from customer state. If you've sent Margaret a deep-clean offer and she said no, the suggestion regenerates every render — guilt-inducing and noisy. The 'Send win-back' action (1906-1911) opens the composer but doesn't mark the suggestion as actioned anywhere. Within a month every customer card is screaming.
findings.fix: Add a `customer_suggestion_state` table or jsonb column with dismissed/snoozed/sent timestamps keyed on suggestion type. Filter at 189-192. Show 'Snoozed (3)' as a collapsible footer. Bonus: ask 'How did it go?' a week after sending — feeds a real outcome dataset.

findings.title: Accessibility: archive/PIN buttons reveal only on :hover, invisible on touch
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:573-585 / 639-651 / 713-725 — all three densities wrap the red X archive button in `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100`. On any touch device there is no hover state, so the button effectively doesn't exist until first tap reveals it (or doesn't, depending on the browser). The 'Coming soon' chip at 1518-1527 also has no role/label for screen readers.
findings.fix: Use focus-within + always-show on touch (`pointer:coarse` media query) or move archive into a row-level overflow menu. Add aria-label='Remove {name}' to the button. This affects every list interaction.

findings.title: Accessibility: native modals trap nothing — Escape, focus loop, aria-modal all missing
findings.detail: MessageComposer (Customers.jsx:786-937), AddCustomerModal (2115-2295) and CustomerImport's ModalShell (404-432) are plain divs with no role='dialog', no aria-modal, no Escape-to-close handler, no focus trap, no initial focus. A keyboard user can tab out of the modal into the underlying page. Screen reader announces nothing on open.
findings.fix: Wrap with a single Modal primitive (you already need it for the duplication noted in the dead-ends report) that handles role='dialog', aria-modal='true', Escape, focus-trap, and restoration of focus to the trigger on close. Standard Radix Dialog or HeadlessUI Dialog drops this in.

findings.title: Accessibility: emoji-only sub-tab label for the most sensitive section
findings.detail: Customers.jsx:1547 — the SecureVault sub-tab renders as just '🔒' with no text label or aria-label, while every other tab is a readable word ('overview', 'history' etc). A screen reader user will hear 'lock' or nothing depending on platform. The most security-sensitive part of the UI is the least labelled.
findings.fix: Always show 'Secure' next to the icon, or add aria-label='Secure vault'. Same applies to the 🔒 emoji used for the 'at limit' state at 2458.

findings.title: Accessibility + colour-only state in many badges
findings.detail: StatusBadge (477-481) and PriorityBadge (485-497) communicate state via colour + a single character ('●' / '◌' / '⚠'). Customer card uses a tiny coloured dot (568) as the sole status indicator in compact density. The amber 'urgent' pulse (559) is the only signal that a customer needs attention. Colour-blind users (~8% of male cleaners) lose meaning.
findings.fix: Always pair colour with text and shape. In compact view, replace the dot with the first letter of status ('A'/'L'/'R') in a coloured pill. Use border-style not just colour for urgent rows.

findings.title: Wow opportunity: postcode clustering / 'Are you near them today?'
findings.detail: Customer list has postcode on every row (681, 617, 562) but never uses it to help the cleaner physically. A real cleaner's day is route-driven. None of the three competitors named (Pinnacle, Deputy, BrightHR) do customer-CRM AND route awareness in one screen. Cadi already has lifetime value + postcode + last_job_date.
findings.fix: Add a 'Near today's jobs' filter that takes the user's scheduled jobs (already in jobs table per dataModel) and shows lapsed/at-risk customers whose postcode prefix matches. One-tap message: 'I'm in SW4 tomorrow — want me to pop in for the windows?'. This is the single feature that would make Cadi viral on the UK cleaning Facebook groups.

findings.title: Wow opportunity: vault unlock should auto-populate the cleaner's day
findings.detail: Today the vault is per-customer and locked. A cleaner with 8 jobs today has to drill in, unlock, read, drive, repeat. The auto-lock countdown (Customers.jsx:1170-1196) is delightful UI but wrong workflow.
findings.fix: Add a 'Today's access' panel on the cleaner's home screen: one PIN entry unlocks the day's stops in order with key/alarm/gate codes ready. Auto-lock between stops. This is the moment cleaners would screenshot and share — 'my app literally hands me the keys for the day'.

findings.title: Dead button: 'Call' has no onClick handler
findings.detail: Confirmed. The Quick action 'Call' button in CustomerDetail renders with no onClick — clicks are silently swallowed. On mobile this should dial via tel:; on desktop it should at least copy the number or open a contact.
findings.fix: Replace with <a href={`tel:${customer.phone}`}> or onClick that triggers navigator.clipboard.writeText(customer.phone) and a toast. Hide the button entirely when !customer.phone.

findings.title: Dead button: 'Add note' has no onClick handler
findings.detail: Confirmed. No handler — the user gets no feedback. Customers.notes is editable via the Edit modal, so the entry point exists but this shortcut just dies.
findings.fix: Wire onClick to open the AddCustomerModal in edit mode focused on the notes field, or add an inline note composer that calls onUpdateCustomer with appended text + timestamp.

findings.title: 'Customer portal' invite is a non-interactive 'Coming soon' badge
findings.detail: Confirmed — purely cosmetic div, not even a disabled button. Misleads users into thinking the feature is shipping. Adds visual noise on every detail view.
findings.fix: Either remove until the portal lands, or wire a real waitlist CTA (logs interest to a feature_requests table). Don't ship vapor UI in production.

findings.title: Archive confirm UI duplicated 3x across density modes
findings.detail: Confirmed: the same {confirmArchive ? <yes/no row> : <x button>} block is copy-pasted across compact/medium/large with only sizing tweaks (w-7 vs w-8 vs w-6). Bug-fix surface multiplies by 3.
findings.fix: Extract <ArchiveAffordance size='sm'|'md'|'lg' onArchive={...} confirm={confirmArchive} setConfirm={setConfirmArchive} /> and reuse. Saves ~40 LOC and prevents drift.

findings.title: GlassCard background+grid texture duplicated inline in 3 modals
findings.detail: Confirmed. MessageComposer, AddCustomerModal, SecureVault locked card, and CustomerImport ModalShell all re-implement the gradient + grid texture overlay inline rather than reusing GlassCard. Any tweak to the design system has to be applied in 4 places.
findings.fix: Promote GlassCard (and a ModalShell wrapper that adds the fixed inset overlay + close button) to src/components/ui/. Replace inline implementations.

findings.title: Hardcoded staff PINs in VAULT_STAFF — fake security
findings.detail: VAULT_STAFF is a hardcoded array with PINs '1234', '5678', '0000' shipped in the client bundle. Any user can View Source on the deployed app and read every PIN. The 'Secure Vault' protects nothing. The comment 'Manager override PIN — change in Settings' is misleading — there is no Settings wiring.
findings.fix: Move PIN verification server-side: hash PINs in the staff table, call a verify_vault_pin edge function that returns a short-lived unlock token. Store vault data in DB (RLS) not localStorage. The current localStorage approach also means access logs are easily wiped and never reach the manager.

findings.title: Vault data and access log stored in localStorage, not DB
findings.detail: vaultDataKey/logKey writes to localStorage. Access logs the manager is told they can see are local to the device that did the viewing — manager on a different machine sees nothing. Data also lost on cache clear / browser switch.
findings.fix: Persist customer_vault and vault_access_log to Supabase with RLS scoped to ownerId. The edge function that verifies PIN should also append the audit row server-side so it can't be tampered with.

findings.title: god-component: Customers.jsx is 2782 lines with 6+ components defined
findings.detail: Single file holds: JOB_TYPES, generateSuggestions, generateMessage, generateAIMessage, GlassCard, SL, Chip, Alert, StatusBadge, PriorityBadge, StarRating, CustomerRow (3 density variants), MessageComposer, SecureVault, CustomerDetail, AddCustomerModal, root CustomerTab. CustomerDetail alone is ~500 LOC with 5 sub-tabs (overview/history/suggestions/messages/secure) plus GoCardless logic. Reviewing or testing is impractical.
findings.fix: Split into src/pages/customers/ with: ui/{GlassCard,Chip,Alert,StatusBadge,PriorityBadge,StarRating}.jsx, CustomerRow.jsx (one component, density prop), CustomerDetail/{index.jsx,OverviewTab,HistoryTab,SuggestionsTab,MessagesTab,SecureVaultTab,GoCardlessPanel}.jsx, MessageComposer.jsx, AddCustomerModal.jsx, suggestions.js (rules engine), messageTemplates.js. Root file drops to <400 LOC.

findings.title: god-component: CustomerDetail mixes 5 unrelated concerns
findings.detail: CustomerDetail owns: suggestions memo, rounds fetch, surveys fetch, jobs+invoices fetch, GoCardless mutation handlers (sync, mandate, collect, mandate-sync), edit modal, vault rendering, tab routing. ~15 useState + 4 useEffect in one component. Any tab change re-renders everything; GoCardless state survives even when the user is on the History tab.
findings.fix: Split per-tab components, each owning its own data fetching via custom hooks (useCustomerJobs, useCustomerSurveys, useGoCardless). Pass customer as prop. Move GC logic into a useGoCardless(customerId) hook.

findings.title: Effect chain: 3 separate useEffects refetch on every customer.id change without coordination
findings.detail: Three independent useEffects keyed on customer.id each cancel/refetch separately. Switching customers fires: surveys query, rounds query, jobs+invoices Promise.all. No loading coordination — UI flashes empty states inconsistently. The surveys effect at 1330 also doesn't refetch when customer changes from non-commercial to commercial.
findings.fix: Consolidate with react-query/SWR (already a Supabase app — install @tanstack/react-query). Failing that, a single useCustomerDetailData(customerId, isCommercial) hook returning {jobs, invoices, surveys, rounds, loading}. Stops the cascade of conditional renders.

findings.title: Errors swallowed silently in 3 effects — user sees nothing
findings.detail: listSurveysForCustomer, listRoundsForCustomer, jobs/invoices Promise.all, and listOpenSurveys all .catch(() => {}). If the DB query fails (RLS misconfig, network), the user sees stale/empty UI with no indication anything is wrong. The jobs catch only sets allJobs=[], indistinguishable from 'no jobs'.
findings.fix: Capture error into state: catch(err => { if (!cancelled) setError(err); }). Render an inline error banner with retry. At minimum log to a telemetry sink. Same fix needed in surveysLoaded effect at 2333.

findings.title: surveysLoaded flag never resets — stale data after import or new survey
findings.detail: useEffect only loads openSurveys once (gated by surveysLoaded). After the user creates a survey, archives a customer, or imports new commercial customers, the strip at 2616 shows stale data until full page reload. Also: switching segmentFilter away and back never refetches.
findings.fix: Drop the surveysLoaded flag, instead key the effect on segmentFilter and add a refresh function passed to children (mirrors refreshCustomers/refreshJobs). Or move openSurveys into DataContext.

findings.title: Prop drilling: onUpdateCustomer/onDeleteCustomer/ownerId threaded through 4 layers
findings.detail: DataContext already exists and owns customers. CustomerTab pulls addCustomer/updateCustomer/deleteCustomer from useData then re-passes them as props to CustomerDetail, which re-passes to AddCustomerModal and StarRating. Each component could useData() directly.
findings.fix: Have CustomerDetail / AddCustomerModal / SecureVault consume useData() and useAuth() directly rather than receiving callbacks. Removes ~6 props per component and eliminates the risk of forgetting to wire a callback.

findings.title: useEffect at 1345 + 1355 race when customer.id changes rapidly
findings.detail: Both effects use a local cancelled flag, which works, but they don't share an AbortController so the underlying Supabase requests still run to completion and consume bandwidth/quota.
findings.fix: Use AbortController and pass signal to supabase queries (or rely on react-query). For now acceptable, but worth flagging given users click-spamming the customer list.

findings.title: handleStartSurvey uses alert() for error feedback
findings.detail: Native alert() is jarring, blocks the thread, can't be styled, and is invisible in many mobile webviews. Inconsistent with the inline Alert component used elsewhere in this file.
findings.fix: Use the existing <Alert type='red'> inline near the survey CTA, or a toast. Mirror the gcError pattern (state + render banner).

findings.title: generateAIMessage assumes data.content[0].text shape without validation
findings.detail: If the edge function returns a different shape (e.g. an error wrapped in data instead of error, or content=[]), this returns '' silently and the composer replaces the user's text with empty. The UI shows no failure — user thinks AI ran but produced blank.
findings.fix: Validate: if (!data?.content?.[0]?.text) throw new Error('AI returned empty response'). The MessageComposer catch at 773 already handles thrown errors via setAiError.

findings.title: MessageComposer catch swallows error details
findings.detail: catch { setAiError('Couldn't generate message — check your connection.') } discards the actual error. If it's a rate-limit or auth issue, the user gets misleading advice to check their connection.
findings.fix: catch (err) { setAiError(err.message || 'Couldn't generate message'); console.error('AI generate failed', err); }

findings.title: GoCardless mutations: no optimistic UI rollback, success state never auto-clears
findings.detail: gcSuccess banner is set on success but there's no useEffect to clear it on customer change — sticks around after navigating to a different customer. Also onUpdateCustomer is fire-and-forget; if it throws, UI shows success but DB never updated.
findings.fix: Reset gcSuccess/gcError when customer.id changes (useEffect dependency). Await onUpdateCustomer and only set success once it resolves.

findings.title: Static templates + AI generation are not unified
findings.detail: Templates are inline strings while AI uses a prompt. Adding a new message type (e.g. 'thank you after job') means editing both the templates object and the MESSAGE_TYPES list. AI 'personalise' button doesn't even use the template body as a starting point.
findings.fix: Centralise message types in a config array: [{ id, label, subject(customer), body(customer), aiPrompt(customer) }]. Composer picks from one source. AI generator augments the static body rather than replacing.

findings.title: Search filter rebuilds the list 3+ times before sorting
findings.detail: filtered useMemo creates a new array via .filter four times, then [...list].sort once more. For thousands of customers (the stated design target) this is wasteful. Stats useMemo at 2395 then filters the unfiltered customers array three more times.
findings.fix: Single pass: reduce/for-loop once over customers, push to bucket arrays for stats (active/lapsed/at-risk) and filtered. Keep useMemo deps the same. Only meaningful at >2k records.

findings.title: py-2.5 rounded-xl border button class repeated ~30+ times
findings.detail: Confirmed via skim. The 'bg-[rgba(153,197,255,0.06)] border border-[rgba(153,197,255,0.15)] ... rounded-xl' pattern is everywhere. Tailwind makes this tolerable but a single tweak to the design system is a 30+ site edit.
findings.fix: Extract <Button variant='ghost'|'primary'|'danger' size='sm'|'md'> into src/components/ui/Button.jsx. Bonus: enforces a11y (type='button' default, disabled handling, focus ring).

findings.title: Hardcoded shared PINs gate sensitive access codes; PII stored in localStorage
findings.detail: SecureVault (lines 940-1030) uses a hardcoded VAULT_STAFF array with PINs '1234', '5678' and a manager override '0000'. Anyone with the page open can read the PINs from the bundle and unlock. Worse, key codes / alarm codes / gate codes / access notes are persisted to localStorage via key `cadi_vault_${ownerId}_${customer.id}` (line 955, 966, 1027) in plain text, so any XSS or shared device exposes property-access credentials for every customer. The access log is also localStorage-only (line 956, 1014), so the 'audit trail visible to manager' is trivially editable/erasable by the staff member and never reaches the server. This is a serious physical-security breach vector (alarm/key/gate codes leak) and a UK GDPR Article 32 failing (no appropriate technical measures).
findings.fix: Move vault data to a server table with RLS scoped to business_id, encrypt at rest with a per-business KMS key, gate access through Supabase Auth roles (not client PINs), and write the access log via an edge function so it cannot be tampered with from the client.

findings.title: No hard delete / right-to-erasure path — 'Remove customer' is a soft archive
findings.detail: Customers.jsx wires both onArchive and onDeleteCustomer to DataContext.deleteCustomer (Customers.jsx lines 2702, 2719) and the UI copy explicitly says 'Their data is kept' (line 1279). The DB layer only flips status='archived' (customersDb.js lines 72-81). UK GDPR Article 17 (right to erasure) requires actual deletion on data-subject request. There is also no export-my-data flow (Article 15/20 SAR) visible in the Customers tab or import wizard, and no consent capture when importing third-party data (e.g. CleanerPlanner CSVs contain names/addresses/phones — there is no lawful-basis prompt).
findings.fix: Add a true hard-delete RPC (cascade jobs/invoices/rounds/surveys for that customer or anonymise the FK rows), expose it from the detail panel as a separate 'Delete forever' action, add a 'Download my data' CSV/JSON export per-customer, and add an import consent checkbox confirming the operator has lawful basis for the source data.

findings.title: Customers table RLS uses owner_id only — no staff/role membership policy
findings.detail: customers_owner_all is the only policy on public.customers and gates on owner_id = auth.uid(). Newer tables (customer_rounds, site_surveys, jobs in 007) use business_id = my_business_id() which supports membership/staff via the businesses helper. That mismatch means: (1) any staff invited via the membership system cannot read customers at all under the strict reading, OR (2) if the app uses the owner's session/service-role anywhere, role gating is invisible to RLS. There is no policy distinguishing owner vs cleaner roles for fields like notes, address, phone, lifetime_value — a junior cleaner with DB access sees everything. No role check appears in customersDb.js either.
findings.fix: Replace customers_owner_all with two policies: (a) customers_business_full for owner / full_access_member using is_full_access_member(business_id), (b) customers_business_limited for cleaner role restricting columns (use a view) — name, address, access notes only for jobs they are assigned to. Backfill business_id on customers and drop owner_id-based gating.

findings.title: Overlapping RLS policies on public.jobs widen access (owner_id OR business_id)
findings.detail: Migration 003 line 79-80 creates jobs_owner_all (owner_id = auth.uid()). Migration 007 line 148-150 creates jobs_business_all (business_id = my_business_id()) on the same table. Postgres OR-combines permissive policies, so a row is visible if EITHER predicate matches. If a row is ever inserted with owner_id set to a user who is no longer a member of the business (e.g. left/transferred), or business_id null but owner_id set, the row leaks via the owner policy. Same risk exists on invoices (003 line 33: invoices_owner_all) which is shown in Customers detail (Customers.jsx line 1367-1372) — invoices have no business_id policy at all and no role gating.
findings.fix: Drop jobs_owner_all once business_id is backfilled. Add invoices_business_all (business_id = my_business_id()) and drop invoices_owner_all. Add an integrity trigger that requires business_id NOT NULL on insert.

findings.title: Customer name leaked in URL query string
findings.detail: handleBookJob navigates to `/scheduler?customer=${encodeURIComponent(customer.name)}`. URLs end up in browser history, server access logs, Sentry breadcrumbs, analytics referers, and any third-party script loaded on /scheduler. Customer names are personal data under UK GDPR. Same pattern is benign for `/survey/${survey.id}` (UUID) but the name should not be in querystring.
findings.fix: Pass customer id (UUID) instead — /scheduler?customerId=... — and resolve the name client-side from DataContext.

findings.title: Full customer PII rows written to console.error on import failure
